entries.definition:
m–s–s to touch, to feel; to cohabit; to hit, to befall, to harm,
to afflict, slight madness, slight fever; to be mean, to be extreme;
thirst-quenching water, verdant pasture; antidote. Of this root.
four forms occur 60 times in the Qur’an: مَسَّ massa 55 times;
يَتَمَاسَّا yatamāssā twice; مَسّ mass twice and مِسَاس misās once.

مَسَّ massa a [v. trans.] 1 to touch (56:79) لَا يَمَسُّهُ إِلَّا الْمُطَهَّرُونَ
none but the purified can touch it 2 to afflict, to befall (38:41) أَنِّي
مَسَّنِيَ الشَّيْطَانُ بِنُصْبٍ وَعَذَابٍ indeed, Satan has afflicted me with
weariness and suffering 3 to consummate a marriage, to have
intercourse (2:237) وَإِنْ طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تَمَسُّوهُنَّ but if you divorce
them before consummating the marriage 4 to comprehend, to

883
